We are seeking a qualified capital/finance professional to lead the capital and control workstream for new regulated insurance entities established by the Group — including DFSA, Cayman, and Lloyd’s structures. The primary focus is on new entity establishment: defining the capital structure, building the financial control framework, and establishing the regulatory reporting infrastructure from inception. Once operational, you will support ongoing financial and regulatory requirements, alongside existing entities and reinsurance programs. You will also supervise a junior analyst. This is a build-focused role for someone comfortable creating systems from scratch, not just managing steady-state reporting.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead financial establishment of new regulated entities (DFSA, Cayman, Lloyd’s) by defining opening balance sheets, capital structures, and financial control frameworks.

  • Establish regulatory reporting frameworks for new jurisdictions (e.g., DFSA, Cayman, Lloyd’s), including reporting packs, calendars, and initial returns.
  • Develop three-statement financial models and entity-level profit/loss structures for new entities.
  • Set up reinsurance program accounting (quota share, excess of loss, ceding commission) and ensure correct accounting treatment.
  • Produce monthly management accounts and reporting packs for new entities; support existing programs as needed.

  • Conduct capital adequacy stress testing and scenario analysis (shock loss, premium growth, FX sensitivity) to assess headroom above SCR/MCR thresholds.

  • Own capital waterfall and inter-entity funding mechanics (dividend upstream, loan repayment, capital repatriation) within approved limits.
  • Track reinsurance counterparty credit exposure, maintaining registers and flagging risks.
  • Produce quarterly board-level capital reporting packs (capital dashboard, solvency headroom, FAL position, stress test outcomes).
  • Support existing entities and reinsurance programs with financial control, reporting, or capital queries.
  • Supervise and direct the Capital & Structuring Analyst on daily tasks.

Preferred Skills

  • Qualified or finalist accountant (ACCA, CIMA, ACA, or equivalent).
  • Experience in capital adequacy, solvency reporting, regulatory returns, and stress testing within insurance/reinsurance.
  • Hands-on financial modeling experience in insurance/reinsurance contexts.
  • Understanding of reinsurance accounting (quota share, excess of loss) and collateral management.
  • Demonstrable experience establishing financial/reporting infrastructure for regulated entities in DFSA/DIFC, Cayman, or Lloyd’s.
  • Strong Xero accounting software skills and advanced Excel/modeling proficiency.
  • Ability to work independently.
summary: Lead capital and control for new regulated insurance entities, building financial frameworks and regulatory reporting. Requires qualified finance professional with insurance/reinsurance expertise.
